################################################################################
# batch_aq.txt — render C1->C2 drain (render campaign batch 1 of ~3)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-03.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool1,3,9,10,12,15 pre-assigned)
#               -> pool0,4,5,6 SKIPPED: leaked in-JVM locks during prior fanouts
#                  (see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock).
#               -> pool7,8 SKIPPED: POISONED during batch_an (in-JVM locks).
#               -> pool2 SKIPPED: real LockException for ao_s2 (2026-06-03) —
#                  poisoned for the shared server's lifetime.
#               -> All six assigned slots opened READ-ONLY CLEANLY during
#                  batch_ap (2026-06-03) — assignment re-verified. pool11/13/14
#                  carry stale-but-tolerable on-disk locks and are the first
#                  fallbacks on a REAL LockException.
# Total RVAs:   156  (first-156 of 458 render-C1 rows: 0x00401690..0x004e7fa0;
#                302 remain for batch_ar/as: 0x004e8030..0x005cb42b)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 26 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj..ap.
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: the gameplay C1->C2 campaign CLOSED 2026-06-03 (al 792->636,
# am->480, an->324, ao->168, ap->0 — ap was 168/168 library_skip RW-Physics).
# render (458) is the LAST big C1 pool. After render: only 8 stragglers (util 3,
# unknown 2, boot/io/save 1 each) + third-party-library residue (kept C1 by
# design). This batch opens the render campaign with the lowest 156 addresses.
#
# *** READ THIS — EXPECT A HIGH library_skip RATE: THIS IS THE RW-CORE BAND. ***
# 148 of the 156 candidates sit in 0x004c0000..0x004e7fff — the RenderWare 3.x
# CORE pages (RwFrame / RwMatrix / RpClump / RpGeometry / RpWorld / registry /
# freelist machinery). Evidence already on file that these pages are vendored:
#   * batch_an confirmed FUN_004e45b0 = RwFrameRemoveChild and FUN_004e6e00 =
#     RpClumpDestroy (BOTH are candidates in this batch — s4 and s6).
#   * ao_s1 reclassed-OUT the RwFrame core family 004c0790..004c15c0 + 004c4220
#     + 004d8bd0 + 004e43b0/004e4440 -> third-party-library[renderware]
#     (re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s1.md has the full idiom list). s1's candidates
#     004c1940/004c1980/004c3e20..004c78e0 and 004d9000/30/40 are page
#     neighbours of that family.
#   * ao_s2 reclassed-OUT the RpClump/geometry page 004e4800..004e8ea0 family
#     -> renderware (re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s2.md). s4/s5/s6 candidates
#     004e4810/004e4860/004e48e0/004e4dd0/004e6760 etc. are their immediate
#     neighbours on the same pages.
# CONSEQUENCE: it is plausible that MOST of the 156 are vendored RW core
# (-> library_skip=<rva>:renderware, kept C1) and the plated count is small.
# That is a SUCCESS, not a failure. Do NOT force-plate vendored code.
# BUT do not blanket-skip by address either: genuine GAME render glue is
# interleaved and known to exist —
#   * s1 has three application-page RVAs (00401690, 004492b0, 00490500) that are
#     ALMOST CERTAINLY genuine game render code (plate them C1->C2 unless the
#     decomp proves otherwise).
#   * the Im2D submit triplet FUN_004cd070/4cd170/4cd140 (cited by ao_s2's hud
#     stroke renderer as its render backend) sits right next to s1 candidates
#     004cd810/004cd850/004cd900/004cdb60 — glue candidates, decide per RVA.
#   * batch_ao found 27 genuine rows inside an 83%-vendored slice. The per-RVA
#     library-confirm rule decides, never the address.
#
# HOW TO DECIDE per RVA (library-confirm rule — apply it AGGRESSIVELY):
#   - If the function decodes as a named RenderWare/CRT primitive, OR Ghidra
#     shows a "Library Function: <sym>" tag, OR it is an obvious thin engine
#     primitive with NO game-state read/write — RW idioms to recognize:
#       * engine-instance fn-table DAT_007d3ff8 (+0x108 alloc / +0x10c free /
#         +0x114 calloc / +0x118 alloc-hint / +0x11c free) with 0x300xx memhints,
#       * plugin-offset globals DAT_007d7xxx, type-pool offset DAT_007d3e70,
#       * module object lists &DAT_00617xxx/&DAT_00618xxx, freelist sentinels,
#       * struct layouts byte-exact vs the vendored gta-reversed RW headers in
#         mashedmod/deps (RwFrame +0x90/+0x94/+0x98/+0x9c/+0xa0; RwMatrix
#         16-dword w/ flags +0x1c; RpClump/RpAtomic/RpGeometry shapes),
#       * refcount AddRef/Release-destroy pairs, RW stream chunk-tag readers:
#       -> do NOT plate it. Put it in the queue row's
#          `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>` field (tag = renderware |
#          RenderWare-Physics-3.7 | msvc-crt). Do NOT rename it on master.
#          Central re-classify reclasses it to third-party-library[<tag>],
#          kept C1.
#   - Only GENUINE game/engine-GLUE that reads/writes game state (0x006xxxxx-
#     0x009xxxxx game globals, entity pools, application-page callers calling
#     INTO it with game data) goes in `rvas=` for the C1->C2 plate. When in
#     doubt about library-vs-glue, PLATE it C1->C2 and record subsystem_observed
#     + a bare [UNCERTAIN] — do NOT silently drop it (ao_s3's 00558b40
#     precedent).
#   - subsystem_observed: plated rows default render, but honor the decomp —
#     game code on these pages may turn out hud/frontend/gameplay glue
#     (ao_s2's font-vector module went gameplay->hud on evidence).
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-03):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_ap fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-2132: 0 plates ALL-SKIP; re-classify commit c16671d1).
#     gameplay C1 = 0 — campaign CLOSED. SAFE to fan out.
#   * Calibrated-band screen over all 156 candidates: ZERO hits in the three
#     CALIBRATED bands (CRT 0x004a0000-0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-
#     0x004fc9e0 — this batch ends 0x004e7fa0, below it | qhull 0x0057c5b0-).
#     The vendored RW-core code in this band is OUTSIDE the calibrated bands
#     and is caught by the per-RVA library-confirm rule.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj..ap
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — the
#     sweep manifest builder returns 0 plated for a bucket otherwise. HARD
#     requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=`). List vendored ones in a SEPARATE
#     `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...` field for the central re-classify (they are
#     NOT plated by the sweep). If a session plates ZERO rows, write `rvas=NONE`
#     (the builder skips the row; the library_skip list is still the
#     deliverable — ap precedent: all six sessions were rvas=NONE).
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section; central re-classify mints from the
#     reserved range (this batch: U-8900..U-9199, S-7000..S-7199 — carried over
#     from batch_ap, which minted NOTHING).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_aq s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for render <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (sibling parallel sessions
#     share the working copy; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FUZZY-GREP GOTCHA (memory; seen in am_s5 + an_s6):
#   A naive `grep <rva> hooks.csv` fuzzy-matches your RVA to unrelated C2 rows
#   that merely CITE it in the notes column. Confirm each candidate's OWN row
#   with an ANCHORED grep `^<rva>,` before deciding it is/isn't drift.
#
# POOL .gpr LOCATION GOTCHA (seen in ap_s3 + ap_s4):
#   The pool .gpr files live FLAT in `mashed_pool\` (project_location =
#   mashed_pool dir root, project_name = Mashed_pool<N>). Do NOT open the
#   NESTED per-slot path mashed_pool/Mashed_pool<N>/Mashed_pool<N>.gpr — that
#   throws a misleading LockException (ap_s4) or NotFoundException (ap_s3).
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (plated total may be well under 156 — expected, not an error).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; honor
#        subsystem_observed; reclass-OUT every library_skip RVA to
#        third-party-library[<tag>] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs from the reserved
#        ranges. render C1 458 -> 302. Then plan batch_ar (render 2/~3,
#        next-156 0x004e8030..; NOTE: that slice brackets the D3DX9-PSGP
#        calibrated band 0x004ec000-0x004fc9e0 — pre-screen it out).
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). pool0/2/4/5/6/7/8 are SKIPPED (see header). If your
#   assigned slot throws a REAL LockException (see the .gpr-location gotcha
#   first!), fall back to ANOTHER verified-free clone (try pool11/13/14 —
#   stale-lock-tolerant — then any fresh clone), and record the ACTUAL slot used
#   in the queue row. ALWAYS program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 156: render (their current hooks.csv label). Per
#   the warning above, EXPECT the dominant share to be vendored RenderWare core
#   (-> library_skip=<rva>:renderware, kept C1) and a small share of genuine
#   game render glue (plate C1->C2; subsystem_observed may be render/hud/
#   frontend/gameplay). CONFIRM per RVA from decomp and record
#   subsystem_observed=<X> / library_skip in the queue row.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_render_00401690_004dc5b0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_00401690_004dc5b0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
Render campaign opener — the scattered low-address render rows + the RW math/
frame pages. THREE application-page RVAs (00401690, 004492b0, 00490500) are the
most likely genuine game render code in the whole batch — read those with extra
care and plate unless proven library. 004c1940..004c78e0 sit on the RwMatrix/
math pages next to ao_s1's reclassed RwFrame family; 004cd810..004cdb60 sit
next to the Im2D submit triplet FUN_004cd070/4cd170/4cd140 (the hud stroke
renderer's backend per ao_s2) — decide per RVA, these may be genuine Im2D glue.
004d9000/30/40 + 004dc300..004dc5b0 are on the 0x004d8xxx-0x004dcxxx registry/
freelist pages (ao_s1 reclassed 004d8bd0 -> renderware). Apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (anchored `^<rva>,` grep — see fuzzy-grep gotcha). Deepen, don't restart.
  Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: ao_s1/ao_s2 fragments (re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s1.md/s2.md) for
  the RW-core idiom lists (DAT_007d3ff8 fn-table, plugin offsets, freelist
  shapes); vendored RW headers in mashedmod/deps.

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (header). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s1";
   use Mashed_pool1 read-only (flat mashed_pool\ location — see gotcha).
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Apply the library-confirm rule. For a genuine-glue RVA: write
   re/analysis/bucket_render_00401690_004dc5b0/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-03) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint). For a vendored RVA: do NOT plate;
   add it to library_skip=<rva>:<tag> in the queue row.
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s1.md: date, aq_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   PLATED only, or NONE>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed +
   library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_aq s1): author <K> C1->C2 plates
   for render 00401690_004dc5b0".

# Candidates (26)
00401690,004492b0,00490500,004c1940,004c1980,004c3e20,004c3e90,004c5e00,004c5f60,004c78a0,004c78e0,004cbca0,004cbd00,004cd810,004cd850,004cd900,004cdb60,004d9000,004d9030,004d9040,004dc300,004dc370,004dc410,004dc4a0,004dc530,004dc5b0

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA decodes as a library family — note in plate-or-queue, do NOT rename; set
  subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva>:<tag>.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_render_004dc750_004e0920  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool3 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire; stale on-disk .lock is
             tolerable — pool3 opened read-only cleanly in ao_s2 + ap_s2. Fall
             back only on a REAL LockException.)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_004dc750_004e0920/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s2.md

# Mission
Second slice (0x004dc750-0x004e0920): the 0x004dcxxx-0x004dfxxx pages (registry/
stream/texture-dictionary territory between the reclassed 004d8bd0 freelist page
and the RpClump core page). EXPECT heavy library_skip[renderware]. Watch for
genuine game glue (TXD/asset loading callers from application pages). Apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s2", Mashed_pool3,
bucket dir bucket_render_004dc750_004e0920/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_aq s2): author <K> C1->C2 plates
for render 004dc750_004e0920".

# Candidates (26)
004dc750,004dc8a0,004dc8e0,004dc970,004dc9e0,004dcb80,004dcbb0,004dcd30,004dcd50,004dd0b0,004dd140,004dd150,004ddcb0,004ddd60,004ddfb0,004de1b0,004de390,004de800,004dee20,004dee40,004df120,004df750,004df870,004df8a0,004e08b0,004e0920

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 3 — bucket_render_004e1290_004e2420  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool9 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_004e1290_004e2420/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s3.md

# Mission
Third slice (0x004e1290-0x004e2420): a dense run of small functions on the
0x004e1xxx-0x004e2xxx pages (26 RVAs in ~0x1200 bytes — getter/setter/iterator
shapes likely; the densest cluster in the batch). EXPECT heavy
library_skip[renderware] (RW core accessors). Apply the library-confirm rule
per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s3", Mashed_pool9, bucket dir
bucket_render_004e1290_004e2420/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s3.md,
commit "scribe(batch_aq s3): author <K> C1->C2 plates for render 004e1290_004e2420".

# Candidates (26)
004e1290,004e12a0,004e12b0,004e13f0,004e14a0,004e15f0,004e1670,004e1710,004e1780,004e17e0,004e18f0,004e1960,004e1990,004e19f0,004e1a90,004e1ac0,004e1b00,004e1b30,004e1b60,004e1c90,004e1ce0,004e1d20,004e1e70,004e2030,004e2090,004e2420

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 4 — bucket_render_004e2470_004e4900  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool10 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire; stale on-disk .lock is
             tolerable — pool10 opened cleanly in an_s6/ao_s4/ap_s4 (ap_s4's
             LockException was the NESTED-path mistake, see gotcha). Fall back
             only on a REAL LockException at the FLAT location.)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_004e2470_004e4900/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s4.md

# Mission
Fourth slice (0x004e2470-0x004e4900): freelist/registry machinery (004e2ff0 is
the freelist-free ao_s1 cited as a library callee) up through the RwFrame
hierarchy page — 004e45b0 is Ghidra-CONFIRMED RwFrameRemoveChild (batch_an) and
004e4450 its sibling; 004e4810/004e4860/004e48e0 neighbour the ao_s2-reclassed
004e4800. EXPECT heavy library_skip[renderware]. Apply the library-confirm rule
per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s4", Mashed_pool10, bucket dir
bucket_render_004e2470_004e4900/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s4.md,
commit "scribe(batch_aq s4): author <K> C1->C2 plates for render 004e2470_004e4900".

# Candidates (26)
004e2470,004e2500,004e2530,004e2650,004e2930,004e2b60,004e2bf0,004e2fa0,004e2ff0,004e3160,004e33c0,004e3410,004e3470,004e4130,004e41b0,004e42a0,004e42d0,004e4300,004e4380,004e4450,004e45b0,004e47b0,004e4810,004e4860,004e48e0,004e4900

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 5 — bucket_render_004e4b90_004e5ef0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool12 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_004e4b90_004e5ef0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s5.md

# Mission
Fifth slice (0x004e4b90-0x004e5ef0): the RpClump/RpAtomic/RpGeometry page
proper — 004e4dd0 neighbours the ao_s2-reclassed 004e4d90; 004e5fc0 (s6) was
cited by ap_s1's 00562660 as a refresh callee. EXPECT heavy
library_skip[renderware]. Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only
genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s5", Mashed_pool12, bucket dir
bucket_render_004e4b90_004e5ef0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s5.md,
commit "scribe(batch_aq s5): author <K> C1->C2 plates for render 004e4b90_004e5ef0".

# Candidates (26)
004e4b90,004e4bd0,004e4c00,004e4c30,004e4dd0,004e4ec0,004e4f70,004e5020,004e5190,004e51e0,004e5280,004e5300,004e55d0,004e5660,004e5680,004e5700,004e5820,004e5840,004e5850,004e5bf0,004e5c30,004e5c70,004e5cd0,004e5d00,004e5d30,004e5ef0

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 6 — bucket_render_004e5f30_004e7fa0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool15 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_render_004e5f30_004e7fa0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth slice (0x004e5f30-0x004e7fa0): the clump/atomic lifecycle tail —
004e6e00's page neighbours (RpClumpDestroy is Ghidra-CONFIRMED, batch_an;
004e6760 neighbours the ao_s2-reclassed 004e6710..004e6fe0 family) plus the
0x004e7xxx handle-helper page (FUN_004e7e30 was an ao_s1 stub note; 004e7d40..
004e7e90 are its siblings). EXPECT heavy library_skip[renderware]. Apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_aq_s6", Mashed_pool15, bucket dir
bucket_render_004e5f30_004e7fa0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_aq_s6.md,
commit "scribe(batch_aq s6): author <K> C1->C2 plates for render 004e5f30_004e7fa0".

# Candidates (26)

# 1/~3). After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header),
